codeReview.summary: The feature adds worktree command wiring, dot shorthand handling, and collision resolution, but `cmd_worktree` does not satisfy the non-git fallback contract: it always emits `script_worktree`, which includes `git worktree add` text even when invoked outside git repositories.

issues.description: `cmd_worktree` always calls `script::script_worktree(...)` and never branches to `script::script_mkdir_cd(...)` for non-git directories. This violates the feature contract/expected behavior requiring non-git worktree invocations to emit mkdir-only scripts (no `worktree add` command text).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation contract and shell spec test expectations are misaligned for non-git worktree behavior, which can allow contract violations to pass tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:100-103 requires `lab worktree` in non-git dirs to emit mkdir output with no `worktree add`; spec/tests/test_12_worktree.sh:25-39 only asserts mkdir presence and explicitly documents the guarded worktree command as acceptable.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When Ruby behavior and validation contract diverge, the current worker procedure lacks an explicit precedence rule to prefer the validation contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: rust-worker SKILL.md asks workers to match Ruby exactly and read fulfills IDs; transcript skeleton states the worker treated guarded `script_worktree` output as correct by citing Ruby behavior even though VAL-CLI-020 requires no `worktree add` in output.

summary: Review failed with one blocking correctness issue: non-git `worktree` output still includes `git worktree add` because `cmd_worktree` never switches to mkdir-only script generation.
